reasoning: The informal statement asks to show the operation $a \star b = a - b$ on $\mathbb{Z}$ is not commutative. By definition, an operation is not commutative iff there exist elements $a, b$ with $a \star b \neq b \star a$, i.e., $a - b \neq b - a$. The Lean statement `∃ a b : ℤ, a - b ≠ b - a` is exactly the negation of commutativity for this operation, on the correct type $\mathbb{Z}$. This is a logically equivalent formalization.
